children.label: A
children.text: Increasing the energy efficiency of vehicles, buildings, and industrial processes.

children.label: B
children.text: Reducing the demand of the United States for energy, especially energy from foreign sources.

children.label: C
children.text: Reducing the cost of energy and making the economy more efficient and competitive.

children.label: D
children.text: Improving the energy security of the United States.

children.label: E
children.text: Reducing the environmental impact of energy-related activities.

children.label: i
children.text: hybrid and electric propulsion systems;

children.label: ii
children.text: plug-in hybrid systems;

children.label: iii
children.text: advanced combustion engines;

children.label: iv
children.text: weight and drag reduction technologies;

children.label: v
children.text: whole-vehicle design optimization; and

children.label: vi
children.text: advanced drive trains;

children.label: B
children.text: cost-effective technologies, for new construction and retrofit, to improve the energy efficiency and environmental performance of buildings, using a whole-buildings approach, including onsite renewable energy generation;

children.label: C
children.text: advanced technologies to improve the energy efficiency, environmental performance, and process efficiency of energy-intensive and waste-intensive industries;

children.label: D
children.text: advanced control devices to improve the energy efficiency of electric motors, including those used in industrial processes, heating, ventilation, and cooling; and

children.label: E
children.text: technologies to improve the energy efficiency of appliances and mechanical systems for buildings in cold climates, including combined heat and power units and increased use of renewable resources, including fuel.
$783,000,000 for fiscal year 2007;
$865,000,000 for fiscal year 2008; and
$952,000,000 for fiscal year 2009.
For activities under , $50,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2007 through 2009.section 16192 of this title
For activities under , $7,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2007 through 2009.section 16195 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: $200,000,000 for fiscal year 2007;

children.label: B
children.text: $270,000,000 for fiscal year 2008; and

children.label: C
children.text: $310,000,000 for fiscal year 2009.
For activities under subsection (a)(2)(D), $2,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2007 and 2008.

children.heading: Extended authorization
children.label: d Extended authorization
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated to the Secretary to carry out $50,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2010 through 2013.section 16192 of this title
the issuance or implementation of energy efficiency regulations;
the weatherization program established under part A of title IV of the Energy Conservation and Production Act ( et seq.);42 U.S.C. 6861
